reasoning: President Tinubu's tax reform bills have been strongly backed by the administration. As of March 18, 2025, the Nigerian House of Representatives officially passed the bills. The Senate has listed the bills as its first agenda item upon resuming on April 29 / May 6, 2025. Given that a consensus on the controversial VAT-sharing formula was reportedly reached by the Nigeria Governors' Forum earlier, the only major hurdles remaining are Senate passage, potential harmonization, and the President's signature. The Presidency confirmed in April 2025 that the bill will exempt real estate, land, and rent from VAT to boost housing affordability—a highly popular aspect of the bill with little opposition compared to the revenue-sharing provisions. With almost two full months remaining before the July 1, 2025 deadline and the bills poised at the finish line, passage and enactment are highly likely. Thus, there is an ~86% chance the bill will be signed into law before the target date.
